laws.title: Public Law 24-178 | Equality in Employment Act
laws.status_basis: SEC. 6(a)(1)-(2) expressly amends 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add gender identity and sexual orientation and to broaden the protected classes.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, preamble, findings, enforcement direction, and conclusion are note material.
provisions.evidence: SEC 1. SHORT TITLE. This Act may be cited as the "Equality in Employment Act."
provisions.notes: These provisions do not create permanent Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Direct amendment to 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add gender identity and sexual orientation as protected classes.
provisions.exact_change: Add gender identity and sexual orientation as protected classes in section 703(a) of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.
provisions.evidence: Amend Section 703(a) of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a)) to include gender identity and sexual orientation as protected classes.
provisions.notes: This is the controlling codification hook.

provisions.text_summary: Broader employment-discrimination amendment expanding the protected classes list.
provisions.exact_change: Expand employment-discrimination protections to include race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, gender identity, and sexual orientation.
provisions.evidence: Expand protections against discrimination to explicitly include race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, gender identity, and sexual orientation.
provisions.notes: Keep as part of the Title VII amendment.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note carries a full-text dump and archive link; the substantive Title VII amendment belongs in Title 42, not as a dump-heavy note.

recommended_actions: Keep the Title 42 amendment in place.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from the note and replace it with a concise statutory note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: SEC. 6(a)(1)-(2) expressly amends 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add gender identity and sexual orientation and to broaden the protected classes.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep the Title 42 amendment in place. | Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from the note and replace it with a concise statutory note.
laws.source_evidence: active keyword `in effect` in list or label | view | https://drive.google.com/file/d/1A6ZbQtPTfFkeQ0Elimx_DLuUE7NAo1Rs/view | 112908796b61971af11613c65b9002f301964ffaf9c6f433130ed7d4c77947f3
laws.exact_change: Add gender identity and sexual orientation as protected classes in section 703(a) of the Civil Rights Act of 1964. | Expand employment-discrimination protections to include race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, gender identity, and sexual orientation.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and findings establish a repeal-and-relief act rather than new permanent Code text.
provisions.notes: These provisions only frame the repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Express repeal of Pub. L. 9-55.
provisions.exact_change: Mark Pub. L. 9-55 as repealed in full.
provisions.notes: This is the controlling operative clause.

provisions.text_summary: Sanctions are declared null and void and lifted on passage.

Keep SEC 3(c) as a concise statutory note.
